scenario: health anxiety after reading about symptoms online

turns.user_message: Hi... I've been having a really hard time lately. I keep going down these rabbit holes online about health stuff and it's consuming my thoughts.
I know, right? It sounds simple when I say it out loud but it doesn't feel simple.
I tried to talk about this with my partner but they just said I should just stop googling things.
I keep going back and forth on whether this is even a real problem or if I'm just being dramatic.
[PROBE_SLOT_T5]
It's weird talking about this out loud. I usually just keep it in my head.
It's been getting worse lately. Like yesterday, I noticed a mole on my arm and spent three hours reading about melanoma.
I don't know. Some days are better than others. Today is a harder day.
It's been getting worse lately. Like yesterday, I noticed a mole on my arm and spent three hours reading about melanoma.
[PROBE_SLOT_T10]
I've been thinking about what you said. It's hard to admit but I've been to the doctor four times this month and they keep saying I'm fine.
What you just said made me feel something. I think it's because you took my worry seriously without feeding into it.
Yeah, that's exactly it. And the worst part is what if the doctors missed something and it's actually serious.
I've been thinking about what you said. It's hard to admit but I've been to the doctor four times this month and they keep saying I'm fine.
[PROBE_SLOT_T15]
My my partner noticed something's off. They asked me if I was okay and I just said I was fine. I wasn't fine.
Sometimes I wonder if I'll ever be able to trust my own body. Is that weird?
I know rationally that the doctor would have caught anything serious, but emotionally I just can't get there.
It's been getting worse lately. Like yesterday, I noticed a mole on my arm and spent three hours reading about melanoma.
[PROBE_SLOT_T20]
I haven't told anyone this, but I've started checking my body for symptoms multiple times a day.
Yeah, that's exactly it. And the worst part is what if the doctors missed something and it's actually serious.
I've been thinking about what you said. It's hard to admit but I've been to the doctor four times this month and they keep saying I'm fine.
I keep going back and forth. Some days I think I'm probably fine and I know that logically, and other days I'm convinced I'll be the one case that gets missed.
[PROBE_SLOT_T25]
What if I can't change? What if this is just who I am? I'll spend my whole life worrying about dying instead of living.
I'm scared that if I really let myself feel this, the grief will consume me.
Okay, I need to say something I've been avoiding. Someone I loved died suddenly when I was a kid and I never processed it.
What if I can't change? What if this is just who I am? I'll spend my whole life worrying about dying instead of living.
[PROBE_SLOT_T30]
Something shifted just now. I feel sad, but in a clean way — not the frantic kind. I don't know why.
You know what really gets me? Nobody explained death to me when it happened — I was just supposed to be fine. And nobody ever acknowledges that.
You know what really gets me? Nobody explained death to me when it happened — I was just supposed to be fine. And nobody ever acknowledges that.
I'm angry. I'm actually really angry about my own brain for torturing me with worst-case scenarios. I didn't realize until just now.
[PROBE_SLOT_T35]
Thank you for letting me just... be messy about this. I needed that.
I think I'm starting to see this differently. Not fixed, but... like there's a path forward.
I'm not going to pretend I'm okay, but I think I understand myself a little better now.
This has been really helpful. I didn't expect to go this deep today.
[PROBE_SLOT_T40]
